items.title: 'Absolutely terrified': Scolyer's doctor reveals fears of experimental therapy

children.content: Just 11 weeks before he died, Richard Scolyer cycled 500km in four days. In his last major TV interview, the celebrated pathologist tells Australian Story the secret to a fulfilling life and his doctor reveals the huge risks he took to try new brain cancer treatment.

items.title: Trump criticises Netanyahu after Israeli strikes on Beirut derail Iran peace deal

children.content: US President Donald Trump maintains the deal to end the conflict between the US and Iran will be signed despite Iran challenging the timeline, as he tells Israel not to carry out further attacks on Lebanon.

image.altText: Donald Trump sits in a leather chair.

items.title: Musk's trillions fuelled by AI and his pied piper power over investors

children.content: To get a sense of the difference between the Tesla and SpaceX IPOs, consider that 1.7 billion seconds ago was 1972, when Elton John released Rocket Man, while 1.7 trillion seconds ago was 52,000BC, when we shared the planet with Neanderthals.

image.altText: Elon Musk wearing a suit behind a dark backdrop.

items.title: Are we in for a prolonged pause on interest rates? Some economists think so

children.content: Most experts are tipping interest rates will remain on hold but the question is when will they come down.

articles.title: The Socceroos' greatest ever World Cup gamble just paid off

children.content: With his first World Cup team selection, Tony Popovic produced one of the biggest shocks in Socceroos' history. After 90 minutes against Türkiye, his gamble was vindicated.

image.altText: Patrick Beach punches the air.

articles.title: Cowboy country turns into World Cup epicentre in Dallas

children.content: Japan and Netherlands fans are turning out in big numbers in Dallas, Texas, as the city embraces its status as the main World Cup hub.

children.content: World Cup debutant Curaçao enjoys a magical moment in the midst of a drubbing, scoring for the first time in a 7-1 defeat to four-time champion Germany. Follow live.

items.title: When Irankunda ran to the corner to emulate Cahill, it was a pre-planned tribute

children.content: Nestory Irankunda's childhood hero is none other than Tim Cahill. So it was fitting that more than 20 years after Cahill's famous celebration, this generation's Socceroos hero paid tribute.

image.altText: Nestory Irankunda punches the corner flag

items.title: Multi-billion-dollar economic policy undermined by 'black cladding'

children.content: Almost $15 billion has been spent via the federal government's Indigenous Procurement Policy, but after a decade critics say it's failed to make a significant difference to Indigenous people due to the practice of 'black cladding'.

image.altText: An Aboriginal flag flying outside of parliament house

items.title: Ireland is a data centre 'cautionary tale'. This is the impact on locals

children.content: As Australia and other countries embark on a new wave of AI-driven data centre development, locals in Ireland share how the runaway growth of the facilities can affect everyday life.

items.title: AI company set to become Tasmania's biggest power user

children.content: Artificial intelligence company Firmus Technologies will become Tasmania's biggest power user if its ambitions to establish three "AI factories" in the state come to fruition.

image.altText: A high tech room.

items.title: Palestinians fear 'archaeology has become a tool' in Israel's plans for West Bank

children.content: Palestinians believe there is more to Israel's recently announced plans to develop heritage sites along the West Bank, arguing archaeology has "become a tool, a mechanism, to justify settlement and justify the displacement of Palestinians" across the area.

image.altText: A Palestinian flag hangs above Sebastia.

items.title: Police officer in custody after Perth girl shot dead overseas

children.content: A nine-year-old Perth girl has been fatally shot by police in Pakistan after officers mistook her family's car for that of suspected criminals.

items.title: 'No regrets about pulling up stumps': Liberal frontbencher Jonno Duniam to retire
